title: 10 best herbal creams for taste changes

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to taste disturbances and improve overall flavor perception.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can alleviate anxiety and stress-related changes in taste, such as metallic or bitter tastes.

Digestive soothing properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help soothe digestive issues like bloating, nausea, and mouth sores that can affect taste and make food unpalatable.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with taste changes because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to soothe and calm the taste buds, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause changes in taste perception. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system can also help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, which can also impact taste. As a result, using Melissa officinalis creams may help restore a person's normal sense of taste.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce anxiety and stress-related taste changes by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, thereby improving the sense of taste.
Limonene: A terpene that aids in reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, which can be disrupted by taste changes, allowing for a more balanced sense of taste.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to taste changes, ultimately promoting a healthier sense of taste.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which contribute to the herb's flavor and aroma.
Flowers: Utilized for their delicate, sweet, and slightly minty flavor, making them suitable for creams aimed at improving taste and digestion.
Stems: Employed for their mild, slightly sweet flavor and potential medicinal properties, which can complement the herb's overall effect on taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in early morning when they are at their highest oil content concentration.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 1 week.
Infuse the dried leaves in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil at a 1:5 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Mix the herbal oil with beeswax and shea butter in a 1:3 ratio to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: This compound helps to stimulate appetite and improve taste by increasing saliva production, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing dry mouth or a lack of interest in food due to medication side effects.

Menthol: Menthol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the oral mucosa, which may contribute to changes in taste perception. By reducing inflammation, menthol can help restore normal taste function.

Rosmarinic acid: This antioxidant compound has been shown to have a protective effect on the epithelial cells of the tongue and mouth, helping to maintain healthy tissue function and potentially mitigating taste changes caused by environmental factors or certain medical conditions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with taste changes because of its natural cooling and refreshing properties. The menthol in peppermint creams stimulates the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for processing chemical irritants, including taste. This stimulation can temporarily alter the way we perceive taste, reducing bitterness and discomfort. By soothing the mouth and throat, peppermint creams can also reduce inflammation and irritation, making it easier to enjoy food and beverages. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing taste changes due to medical conditions or treatments.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: A terpene with an analgesic and anesthetic effect, which may help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with taste changes.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the taste buds and mucous membranes from oxidative stress and inflammation.
Linalool: A terpene with a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which may help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to taste changes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which are responsible for the cooling sensation and minty flavor.
Rhyzomes: They are a rich source of menthol and other compounds that give the herb its distinctive flavor and aroma.
Stems: While not as concentrated as leaves or rhyzomes, the stems of Mentha x piperita still contain menthol and other compounds that contribute to its flavor and aroma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup fresh leaves and flowers of mentha x piperita at dawn on a sunny day for best flavor.
Steep the harvested leaves and flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to release oils.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 5 minutes.
Combine the strained liquid mixture with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and whip until thickened to a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat caused by radiation therapy or chemotherapy, leading to improved taste perception.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, thus preserving their ability to detect sweet tastes.

Phytoestrogens: Licorice root extract contains phytoestrogens, which can help balance hormonal fluctuations that may contribute to changes in taste perception, such as those experienced during menopause or pregnancy.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with taste changes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, which can become inflamed due to various conditions such as Sjögren's syndrome or radiation therapy. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, the cream can help to restore a normal sense of taste and reduce bitter or metallic tastes that can be associated with taste changes. This can improve overall quality of life for individuals experiencing taste changes.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin helps to alleviate taste changes by inhibiting the activity of 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme that regulates the balance of cortisol in the body, which in turn can affect taste perception.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the oral cavity and protect against oxidative stress, both of which can contribute to taste disturbances.

Licopyranocoumarin: Licopyranocoumarin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and protect the oral mucosa, reducing irritation and inflammation that can lead to changes in taste.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are widely used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate bitter taste and other taste changes.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their secondary compounds that assist in reducing bitterness and other taste imbalances, promoting a balanced taste experience.
Seeds: The seeds are sometimes used in creams to enhance the flavor and help alleviate taste changes, though they are less commonly used compared to roots and barks.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a 500 milliliter beaker using ethanol 70%.
Combine the extracted roots with 1 liter of distilled water in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a paper filter into a separate container and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant bowl and melt over low heat.
Combine the filtered liquid with the melted wax mixture and stir until a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which can alleviate unpleasant sensations associated with taste changes.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to taste alterations, by neutralizing free radicals and promoting overall oral health.

Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin present in chamomile can bind to GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect that can help reduce anxiety and discomfort related to taste changes, allowing for more comfortable eating and drinking.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with taste changes because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm the mucous membranes in the mouth. This can reduce swelling and irritation, allowing taste buds to function normally again. The chamomile's calming effects also help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can be contributing factors to taste changes. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with taste changes by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of sensory neurons that process taste information.

Matricaria chamomilla terpenoids: These terpenoids, including cis-chrysanthenone and alpha-bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate taste changes by protecting the taste buds from damage and promoting healing.

Apigenin-7-glucoside: This flavonoid glycoside has been shown to have a protective effect on the taste buds, reducing the damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to taste changes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of apigenin and luteolin, which provide a calming and soothing effect on the taste buds.
Seeds: Utilized for their ability to regulate gut health and digestion, which can help alleviate taste changes caused by digestive issues.
Leaves: Employed for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat that may contribute to taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with a sterile scissors to ensure cleanliness.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids after 30 minutes of straining.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil mixture and melt it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes creamy, taking about 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula, the active compound in pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with taste changes, such as sore mouth, throat, and tongue.

Antibacterial property: Pot marigold's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to unpleasant tastes or odors, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating symptoms of bad breath, gum disease, and other conditions.

Wound-healing property: Calendula's ability to promote wound healing can help speed up the recovery process for mouth sores, ulcers, or injuries that may be causing taste changes, allowing for a smoother and more pleasant oral experience.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with taste changes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth. This helps to reduce irritation and inflammation, which can lead to altered taste. By promoting a healthy oral environment, Calendula creams support the restoration of normal taste function, allowing individuals to experience their favorite foods and flavors with renewed enjoyment and appreciation. remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate taste changes by reducing inflammation in the mouth and throat.
Naringenin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect against taste bud damage and reduce oxidative stress, potentially alleviating taste changes.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth and throat, potentially alleviating taste changes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to help alleviate symptoms of mouth sores, sore throats, and other taste-related issues.
Leaves: Used for their antibacterial and antifungal properties to help combat infections and promote a healthy oral environment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh calendula flowers on a sunny day to ensure potency and quality.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve properties.
Infuse 200 grams of the dried flowers in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 200 milliliters of the herbal extract with 800 milliliters of distilled water and 200 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with taste changes.
Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties may help protect the mucous membranes in the oral cavity from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to taste disturbances.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall when the plants are dormant.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea angustifolia root in 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth consistency.
Stir the mixture and pour it into a container, allowing it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used to make creams for taste changes due to their high concentration of silymarin, which can help alleviate liver-related taste disturbances.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 200 grams of dried silybum marianum with 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Stir in 200 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of jojoba oil into the melted beeswax mixture to create a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a clean container for storage and use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees on a dry sunny day.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or jojoba.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids.
Mix 10 milliliters of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water to create a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil from dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves by steam distillation for 2 hours.
Combine 1 liter of distilled water with 10 grams of the extracted essential oil in a stainless steel bowl.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the bowl and heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with taste changes because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and stems and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris with 20 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 10% hydrastis canadensis root extract, coconut oil, beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Add the melted beeswax to the coconut oil and stir until well combined then cool to 70°f over 1 hour.
Mix in the hydrastis canadensis root extract and vitamin e oil, stirring well to ensure even distribution.
Pour the mixture into a clean sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with taste changes because of its unique blend of herbs that support digestive health. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and dark place for at least 14 days to achieve optimal potency.
Grind the dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric spice grinder.
Combine 1 part of the powdered root with 6 parts of petroleum jelly or coconut oil and mix well to create a smooth paste.
Fill small jars with the prepared mixture and seal them tightly before storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

Seeds: Containing high amounts of sesquiterpenes, yarrow seeds have natural antibacterial properties that can help combat bad breath and improve oral health, ultimately enhancing the sense of taste.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered achillea millefolium with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at a gentle heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the liquid extract and stir until fully incorporated, then pour into tin containers and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to mature for 2 weeks before use, shaking the container daily, or store in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with taste changes because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the senses. The herb's citrusy aroma and sweet flavor profile have a gentle yet profound impact on the brain's processing of taste information, allowing individuals experiencing changes in their sense of taste to re-calibrate and regain a normal appreciation for flavors. Additionally, lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the mouth and throat, further contributing to improved taste perception.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, tongue, and throat, which is often associated with taste disturbances caused by conditions like sinusitis or oral cancer treatment.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena creams can help protect the taste buds from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to changes in taste perception.

Soothing and anti-spasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's soothing and anti-spasmodic compounds can help calm down inflammation and spasms in the throat, mouth, and tongue, which can alleviate symptoms of taste disturbances such as dryness, soreness, or burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are a rich source of antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe digestive issues and improve overall gut health.
Seeds: With their high content of unsaturated fatty acids and omega-3 fatty acids, seeds are used to create creams that can alleviate symptoms of taste disorders such as metallic or bitter tastes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems for optimal flavor profile.
Combine the dried herb with 8 ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 4 hours, allowing the oil to infuse with the herbal essence.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy for a shelf-stable preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with taste changes because they contain powerful antioxidants and flavonoids that stimulate the taste buds, revitalizing their function. The cream's natural ingredients also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can dull the sense of taste. Additionally, elder creams can soothe an irritated tongue and palate, allowing flavors to come through more clearly. This results in a restored ability to enjoy a wide range of foods and drinks, making mealtimes a more enjoyable experience.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by altered tastes.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial activity: The antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds in elder cream can help protect against oral infections and damage to the mucous membranes, which can contribute to changes in taste perception.

Mucilage-forming properties: Elder cream's mucilage content forms a protective coating on the mucous membranes, soothing and lubricating them, which can help reduce irritation and discomfort associated with altered tastes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms the digestive tract, making it ideal for treating taste changes.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the elder plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of taste changes, such as inflammation in the mouth and throat.
Barks: Elder barks contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them beneficial for treating taste changes caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried sambucus nigra flowers and mix 10 grams with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Combine the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the wax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into clean glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before labeling and storing for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with taste changes because they can calm the senses and soothe the nervous system. Lavender's calming properties can reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for taste disturbances. The cream's aroma can also stimulate the brain's limbic system, associated with emotions and memory, helping to rebalance the sense of taste. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, further contributing to improved taste perception.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to taste changes and improve overall oral health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream may help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate symptoms of altered taste perception and improve appetite.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's anxiolytic and calming properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for changes in taste and smell perception, promoting a sense of relaxation and improving overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce symptoms associated with taste changes.
Seeds: Lavender seeds have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues, including changes in taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has dried but before midday heat sets in and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 50ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours at room temperature allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Blend 10ml of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 40ml of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl heating gently over low heat until melted and fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with taste changes because of its natural anxiolytic properties, which calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related taste disturbances. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to ease digestive discomfort and alleviate the bitter or metallic tastes that often accompany taste changes. By soothing the digestive tract and calming the mind, fennel creams can restore a sense of balance to one's taste buds, allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods once again.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel cream's carminative properties help to reduce flatulence, bloating, and discomfort associated with taste changes, allowing for a more pleasant eating experience.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Fennel's digestive enzymes stimulate the production of saliva and gastric juices, which can improve appetite, digestion, and nutrient absorption, thereby enhancing flavor perception and enjoyment of food.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce inflammation in the mouth, tongue, and throat, which can contribute to changes in taste perception and alleviate discomfort associated with taste disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fennel are used to make medicinal creams as they contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, leading to a change in taste perception.

Seeds: Fennel seeds are used to make medicinal creams due to their carminative properties, which help to alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion, allowing the senses to focus on other flavors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds from reputable sources for optimal potency and quality.
Soak the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in 4 cups of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 8 hours.
Decoct the soaked foeniculum vulgare seeds in a double boiler with 2 cups of distilled water at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix the herbal liquid extract with 6 ounces of beeswax and 10 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated and melted for a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with taste changes because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for transmitting sensory information related to taste and smell. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to altered taste perception. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties may help protect the taste buds from oxidative damage, allowing them to function more effectively and restoring a sense of normal taste.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the tongue and mouth, which can help alleviate bitter or metallic tastes.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle creams have high antioxidant activity, which helps protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of unpleasant taste changes.

Stomach-soothing properties: Stinging nettle has natural anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe digestive issues such as heartburn, indigestion, and acid reflux, which are often associated with taste changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in minerals and antioxidants, nettle roots help to balance and regulate taste buds.
Rhyzomes: Containing natural antibacterial properties, rhyzomes support healthy oral flora and reduce bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of urtica dioica, also known as nettle, in the early morning or evening to ensure maximum potency and safety.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 2-3 hours or by air drying them for several days until they become brittle and crumbly.
Grind 1 part dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure optimal extraction of the active compounds.

Mix the powdered nettle with 2 parts coconut oil and 1 part beeswax in a small saucepan, heating it gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the mixture melts and emulsifies.

Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring the creamy mixture into sterilized glass jars and allowing it to set at room temperature for several hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with taste changes because they coat and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract. The slippery texture of marshmallows lubricates dry, irritated areas, allowing for a smoother flow of saliva and reducing discomfort associated with altered taste sensations. Additionally, herbal ingredients like licorice root and ginger may help calm inflammation and reduce irritation, further enhancing the relief provided by the creamy texture.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow creams contain demulcent agents that form a soothing film on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by taste changes due to mouth sores, radiation therapy, or other conditions.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow creams help reduce swelling and irritation in the oral cavity, which can be exacerbated by taste changes, allowing for more comfortable eating and swallowing.

Soothing agent: Marshmallow creams contain soothing agents that calm irritated mucous membranes and provide a protective barrier against further irritation, making them easier to eat and swallow even with altered tastes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated tissues in the mouth and throat.
Buds: The buds are used because they have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective at reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by taste changes.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried marshmallow root powder 500mg and sweet almond oil 100ml.
Heat the sweet almond oil in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Add the dried marshmallow root powder to the heated oil and stir continuously for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the mixture steep for at least 8 hours or overnight before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the strained liquid to clean glass containers fill to the top and seal leaving no headspace before storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for taste changes are topical creams or ointments infused with herbs and other natural ingredients that help alleviate temporary or chronic taste disturbances. They work by stimulating taste buds and improving the sense of taste, often due to reduced inflammation or irritation in the mouth and tongue. Examples include creams with ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus, which have been shown to improve taste and enhance quality of life for individuals experiencing taste changes due to medication, illness, or surgery.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to taste changes that are treated with herbal creams are often related to digestive issues and skin problems. For example, herbal creams may be used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as bloating and gas. Other conditions like eczema, acne, and rosacea can also benefit from topical herbal treatments, which can help soothe and calm the skin while reducing inflammation and redness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with taste changes is a blend of Sage, Peppermint, and Thyme. Sage cream helps to soothe and calm the taste buds, reducing inflammation. Peppermint cream stimulates saliva production, which aids in digestion and neutralizes bitter tastes. Thyme cream, rich in antioxidants, promotes healing and reduces inflammation in the mouth and throat. Together, these creams can help restore taste and reduce discomfort associated with taste changes.
